pattern: As two prefixes grow one index at a time, only the two freshly-revealed values can newly become shared, so a per-value appearance counter lets the running overlap size be updated in O(1) per step instead of recomputing an intersection from scratch.
hints: When the prefix grows by one index, the only values that could newly count as 'shared' are the two values just revealed, one from each array.
Keep a counter per possible value of how many of the two arrays have shown it so far in their prefix; a value becomes newly shared exactly when that counter first reaches two.

Maintain a single running total of shared values, bump it whenever a value's counter crosses from one to two, and append the running total to the result after processing both new elements at each index.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible permutation, a single value
two values given in swapped order
identical order in both arrays, so the shared count grows by one at every step
exactly reversed order, so almost nothing is shared until the final index
a small case with partial overlap building unevenly across the prefix
maximum length permutation with one array a full reversal of the other

stress.description: two independently shuffled permutations at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    A = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(A)
    B = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(B)
    return [A, B]

stress.description: smaller shuffled permutations for a second independent random pattern
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    A = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(A)
    B = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(B)
    return [A, B]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def findThePrefixCommonArray(self, A, B):
        n = len(A)
        result = []
        seen_a = set()
        seen_b = set()
        for i in range(n):
            seen_a.add(A[i])
            seen_b.add(B[i])
            result.append(len(seen_a & seen_b))
        return result
